title: Insertion Sort
description: Given an array of integers nums, implement the insertion sort algorithm to sort the array in non-decreasing order. The algorithm works by dividing the input list into a sorted and an unsorted region, then repeatedly taking an element from the unsorted region and inserting it into the correct position in the sorted region.

examples.explanation: Starting with the first element (5), it is already in the sorted region. Next, take 2 from the unsorted region and insert it before 5. Continue this process until all elements are inserted into their correct positions, resulting in the sorted array: [1, 2, 5, 5, 6, 9].

Begin with the first element (12). Next, 11 is taken and inserted before 12. The process continues with 13 being correctly placed after 12, followed by 5 being inserted at the beginning, and finally 6 right after it, leading to the sorted array: [5, 6, 11, 12, 13].

Start with 3 in the sorted region. Insert 0 before it, then 2 between 0 and 3. Next, -1 is inserted at the start. Finally, place 4 after 3 and 5 at the end, giving the sorted array: [-1, 0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5].

constraints: The array length n is in the range [1, 1000].
The values of nums are in the range [-10^3, 10^3].
hints: Consider how you would insert each element from the unsorted part into the sorted part.
Remember that insertion sort is efficient for small data sets or partially sorted data.
Think about how you can use nested loops for comparing and shifting elements.
Try to visualize the process of sorting by drawing the array and the current state of the sorted portion.
